reasoning: The rolling 360-day average threshold to beat is approximately 11.25 events (i.e., we need 12 or more events in the 30-day window). While ACLED event distributions are typically right-skewed (making most random 30-day slices fall below the mean), October 2025 falls directly into an intense hot-phase. The Congolese army (FARDC) launched an "expanded counteroffensive" starting in late September / October 2025, heavily leaning on newly acquired drone capabilities and aerial strikes against M23 positions in the Kivus. Confirmed remote violence events include multiple drone strikes on Sept 27, Sept 29 (hitting at least 5 positions), Oct 15, Oct 21 (multiple sites in Masisi and Walikale), and Oct 24. Since ACLED logs separate locations and days as individual events, alongside the baseline mortar/artillery exchanges of the ongoing war and heavy ADF IED activity, October is highly likely to significantly exceed 12 events. Therefore, the November 2nd resolution date (covering Oct 3 - Nov 2) has a very high probability of resolving YES. The late November date remains high, though there is minor risk of de-escalation surrounding a tentative Nov 13, 2025 peace deal signing. For the January 2026 date, uncertainty increases significantly as offensives may culminate or pause, pushing the estimate closer to 0.5. However, it remains above the generic base rate because FARDC’s structural integration of armed drones represents a dedicated increase in 'Explosions/Remote violence' capabilities over previous years.
